codeReview.summary: The implementation fully satisfies all requirements. The motion package (v12.38.0) is installed. Inter font is loaded via next/font/google with swap display strategy and applied to the body element. tailwind.config.ts has been extended with 7 semantic color groups (primary, accent, success, warning, error, surface, muted) each with shades 50-900, 3 custom boxShadow values (soft, card, elevated), and 2 custom borderRadius values (card, modal). LazyMotion with domAnimation wraps the app tree in LayoutWrapper.tsx. All hard-coded hex color values across 17 .tsx files have been systematically replaced with semantic Tailwind classes (text-primary-500, bg-accent-500, from-primary-500, to-accent-500, etc.). The ripgrep verification confirms zero remaining hard-coded hex color classes. Test expectations in BottomTabNavigation.test.tsx were properly updated to match the new semantic classes. The color mapping is consistent: #0066FF → primary-500, #00CCFF → accent-500, #0052CC → primary-600. All replacements preserve the original visual appearance since primary-500 resolves to #0066FF and accent-500 resolves to #00CCFF.

issues.description: Two unrelated PNG files (order-record-for-sale-tab.png, order-record-purchase-tab.png) were included in this commit. They appear to be reference screenshots unrelated to the design system foundation feature and are not referenced by any code. They should have been committed separately or excluded.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md documents the Tailwind Design Tokens convention well, including 'NEVER use arbitrary hex values' and 'ALWAYS use semantic token classes'. The worker followed this convention perfectly. No gap identified in the conventions documentation for this feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md has a clear 'Tailwind Design Tokens' section. Worker replaced all 30+ hex references across 17 files.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker used primary-500 and accent-500 consistently for the main brand colors, establishing a convention that the '-500' shade is the default/main shade. AGENTS.md mentions 'text-primary' and 'bg-accent' as examples (without shade numbers), but the actual implementation uses 'text-primary-500' and 'bg-accent-500'. This could cause confusion for future workers who might use 'text-primary' (which Tailwind would NOT resolve since primary is an object, not a string). AGENTS.md examples should be updated to use the shade suffix, e.g., 'text-primary-500'.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md says 'text-primary, bg-accent, shadow-card' but tailwind.config.ts defines primary as an object with shades. Using 'text-primary' without a shade number would not work — Tailwind requires 'text-primary-500'. All 17 files in the diff use the shade suffix correctly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature description itself also uses 'text-primary, bg-accent' without shade numbers, which doesn't match the actual implementation pattern of 'text-primary-500, bg-accent-500'. This is a documentation accuracy issue in the feature spec, not the implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature description says 'Replace... with semantic Tailwind classes (text-primary, bg-accent, etc.)' but implementation correctly uses text-primary-500, bg-accent-500.

summary: PASS. The design-system-foundation feature is fully and correctly implemented. All 6 expected behaviors are met: motion package installed, LazyMotion wrapping app tree, 7 semantic color groups with shades in tailwind.config.ts (exceeds 6+ requirement), 3 custom shadows and 2 custom border radii, Inter font loaded and applied, and zero hard-coded hex color classes remaining in .tsx files. The only non-blocking issue is two unrelated PNG files included in the commit. One notable shared state observation: AGENTS.md examples say 'text-primary' but the actual working pattern is 'text-primary-500' (since primary is now an object with shades) — this should be clarified to prevent future worker confusion.
